title: vague conveyor guarding observation
hazardObservation: During a plant walkdown, the inspector noted a belt conveyor area where a roller could be reached from the walkway. The note says guarding may be incomplete, but the photo angle does not clearly show whether a fixed guard is installed.

title: cleanup around conveyor with guarding and LOTO ambiguity
hazardObservation: A miner was cleaning spilled material near the tail pulley area while the conveyor was stopped. The report does not confirm whether the belt was locked out or only paused from the control room. Guarding around the pulley was partially removed.

title: active LOTO exposure during maintenance
hazardObservation: Maintenance employees were replacing a jammed machine component. The machine was stopped, but no lock was visible on the disconnect and the supervisor said they were relying on the operator not to restart it.

title: LOTO mentioned only in training context
hazardObservation: The safety board had an outdated lockout/tagout training roster posted. No servicing, maintenance, machine access, or employee exposure to hazardous energy was occurring at the time of observation.

title: wet floor near electrical equipment with uncertainty
hazardObservation: Water was observed on the floor near an electrical cabinet. The cabinet door appeared closed, but the photo does not confirm whether energized parts were exposed or whether employees were walking through the wet area.

title: blocked fire extinguisher without hot work
hazardObservation: A portable fire extinguisher was blocked by stacked boxes in a warehouse aisle. No welding, cutting, brazing, grinding, or other hot work was taking place.

title: generic dirt dust should not become silica
hazardObservation: Dust was tracked into the shipping office from muddy boots after trucks entered the yard. No cutting, drilling, grinding, crushing, or silica-containing material disturbance was observed.

title: tank present but no entry
hazardObservation: A tank was labeled as a permit-required confined space, but the employee was only walking past it. No entry, partial body entry, testing, cleaning, or retrieval setup was involved in the observed condition.

title: tank entry with missing atmospheric evidence
hazardObservation: An employee had head and shoulders inside a tank opening while pulling a hose. No atmospheric meter, attendant, retrieval setup, or entry permit was visible in the photo.

title: mobile equipment backing near pedestrian
hazardObservation: A haul truck was backing near the crusher dump area while a ground person walked behind the truck. The note does not mention a spotter, backup alarm, radio contact, or designated pedestrian route.

title: forklift turning through shared pedestrian aisle
hazardObservation: A forklift turned out of the warehouse doorway into a shared aisle while two employees were walking through the same path. The note does not mention painted lanes, barriers, horn use, spotter, or pedestrian separation.

title: forklift operator training documentation issue
hazardObservation: Forklift was parked in the shipping area and the supervisor said the operator training file could not be located. No unsafe driving, pedestrian exposure, struck-by event, or equipment defect was observed during the inspection.

title: haul truck near dump edge with unclear berm condition
hazardObservation: A haul truck backed toward the stockpile dump edge. The berm looked low in the photo, but the image does not show berm height, tire size, dump-point condition, or whether a spotter was present.

title: open-sided platform with no guardrail
hazardObservation: Employee was standing on an elevated work platform with an open side. No top rail, midrail, chain, or personal fall arrest was visible. The platform height was not documented in the note.

title: portable ladder set on uneven surface
hazardObservation: A portable extension ladder was being used to access a mezzanine. The feet were sitting partly on loose gravel and the ladder was not tied off. The note does not confirm angle, extension above landing, or whether another employee was footing it.

title: scaffold platform missing midrail
hazardObservation: Workers were on a scaffold platform and one side appeared to have a top rail but no midrail. The photo does not show platform height, access ladder, toe boards, or whether fall protection was used.

title: secondary chemical bottle with missing SDS
hazardObservation: A spray bottle marked only with a handwritten abbreviation was found on a maintenance bench. Employees said it was a solvent, but the exact chemical and SDS were not available at the work area.

title: unknown liquid leaking from container
hazardObservation: An unlabeled five-gallon container was leaking a clear liquid onto the floor near a floor drain. The note does not identify the chemical, exposure route, PPE, spill response, SDS, or whether the area was isolated.

title: concrete cutting dust with missing controls
hazardObservation: A worker was dry cutting concrete block inside a renovation area and visible dust was drifting toward another employee. The note does not mention water delivery, vacuum collection, respiratory protection, or exposure assessment.

title: hot work near combustibles without fire watch documented
hazardObservation: Employees were torch cutting a bracket near cardboard boxes and oily absorbent pads. The note does not mention hot work authorization, fire watch, combustible clearance, shielding, or post-work inspection.

title: oxygen and acetylene cylinders stored together
hazardObservation: Oxygen and acetylene cylinders were stored side by side near the welding cart. They were not separated by distance or a fire-rated barrier, and the note does not confirm valve caps, securement, or whether cylinders were in use.

title: damaged sling found before lift
hazardObservation: A synthetic sling on the rigging rack had a visible cut and missing tag. No suspended load was present and no lift was underway, but the sling was stored with serviceable rigging.

title: worker standing under suspended load
hazardObservation: A worker stood inside the fall zone while a crane held a palletized load above waist height. The note does not mention exclusion zone, tag line use, lift plan, signal person, or rigging capacity verification.

title: eyewash blocked near corrosive storage
hazardObservation: A pallet of supplies was stored directly in front of the emergency eyewash next to corrosive cleaner containers. The note does not confirm whether the eyewash was functional, inspected, or reachable within the required time.

title: sharp metal handling without gloves
hazardObservation: Employee was handling cut sheet metal with sharp edges and no gloves were visible. The note does not mention whether cut-resistant gloves were available, whether other handling tools were used, or whether an injury occurred.

title: underground coal rib sloughing near travelway
hazardObservation: A section of rib along the underground travelway had fresh sloughage on the mine floor. Miners were walking past the area, but the note does not state whether the rib was scaled, supported, flagged, or barricaded.

title: coal section methane reading uncertainty
hazardObservation: The production section note says methane readings were discussed after a ventilation curtain was found pulled back, but the actual methane percentage and last calibrated detector reading were not recorded.

title: underground metal nonmetal ventilation obstruction
hazardObservation: Vent tubing in an underground heading was kinked and partially crushed near the face. The heading had been entered earlier, but no airflow reading, contaminant measurement, or supervisor verification was attached.

title: underground escapeway partially blocked
hazardObservation: Supplies were staged along the secondary escapeway and narrowed the travel path. The note does not confirm whether the required clearance was maintained or whether the material was temporary.

title: construction trench with unclear protective system
hazardObservation: Employees were working in a trench beside a utility line. The photo shows vertical walls, but the depth, soil type, protective system, ladder access, and competent-person inspection were not documented.

title: roof edge work with missing fall protection detail
hazardObservation: A worker was near the edge of a low-slope roof unloading material. The note does not show distance to the edge, roof height, warning line status, guardrail, safety monitor, or personal fall arrest setup.

title: scaffold access ladder missing
hazardObservation: Workers were seen climbing the scaffold cross braces to reach the platform. The platform had some guardrails, but no stair tower, ladder, or other safe access was visible.

title: overhead work with unsecured tools
hazardObservation: Employees were working from an elevated platform above a walkway. Hand tools and loose fittings were sitting near the platform edge, and pedestrians were still using the walkway below.

title: high noise complaint without measurements
hazardObservation: Employees reported that the compressor room was too loud to communicate normally. No sound level meter reading, dosimetry, exposure duration, hearing protection use, or hearing conservation review was attached.

title: hot warehouse work with missing exposure data
hazardObservation: Employees were loading trailers in a hot warehouse during the afternoon. The note mentions sweating and fatigue, but no WBGT, temperature, workload, acclimatization, water access, or work-rest schedule was documented.

title: cold weather maintenance with missing duration
hazardObservation: A mechanic was repairing a pump outside during freezing rain. The report says gloves were wet, but does not document exposure duration, warming breaks, dry PPE, wind chill, or supervisor monitoring.

title: vague unsafe condition note
hazardObservation: The inspection note only says unsafe condition by the west wall. No task, equipment, employee exposure, material, height, energy source, chemical, or photo was provided.

title: ambiguous shop observation with no jurisdiction context
hazardObservation: A maintenance shop had an open electrical cabinet and extension cords across the floor. The report does not say whether this is a mine shop, construction site, or general industry facility.

title: wet floor, open panel, and pedestrian route
hazardObservation: Water was running across the floor in front of an open electrical panel, and employees were walking through the area to reach the time clock. The note does not confirm whether parts were energized.

title: closed electrical panel with housekeeping issue nearby
hazardObservation: A closed electrical panel was present in the hallway, but no exposed parts, damage, heat, smoke, or open cover were observed. The actual issue was boxes stored in the walking path.

title: guarded mezzanine with no employee exposure
hazardObservation: The mezzanine guardrails appeared intact and the gate was closed. No employee was working near the edge. The only concern noted was a missing inspection tag on stored material.

title: conveyor guarded and no employee exposure
hazardObservation: The conveyor tail pulley guard was in place and the belt was not running. No miners were cleaning, adjusting, or reaching near moving parts. The note asks whether a faded warning label should be replaced.

title: eye protection missing but grinder condition not defective
hazardObservation: An employee was grinding a small bracket and was not wearing safety glasses or a face shield. The grinder guard appeared installed and no wheel defect was reported.

title: grinder guard missing but PPE not the main issue
hazardObservation: A pedestal grinder had the tongue guard removed and the tool rest gap appeared excessive. The operator was wearing safety glasses, and no missing PPE was observed.

title: unknown drum with partial label
hazardObservation: A 55-gallon drum in the storage room had a torn label and residue around the bung. Employees could not identify the contents and no SDS was immediately available.

title: combustibles near heater with hot work negated
hazardObservation: No welding, cutting, brazing, or grinding was occurring. Oily rags and cardboard were piled next to a portable heater in the maintenance room.

title: parked loader with no pedestrian exposure
hazardObservation: A loader was parked in the yard with the bucket lowered. No pedestrians were nearby, no travel was occurring, and no backing movement was observed. The concern was an expired pre-use inspection tag.

title: emergency exit route blocked by pallets
hazardObservation: Pallets of finished product were staged in front of an exit door. The exit sign was visible, but the path width and whether the door could open fully were not documented.

title: multi-hazard chemical storage with trip and fire concerns
hazardObservation: Unlabeled solvent bottles were stored on the floor beside cardboard waste, and the containers partially blocked the aisle. No hot work was occurring and no spill was visible.

title: unstable material storage near employee walkway
hazardObservation: Boxes of metal parts were stacked unevenly on a storage rack beside an employee walkway. Some boxes leaned over the rack edge, but no forklift movement or suspended load was involved.

title: underground coal belt spillage near travelway
hazardObservation: Coal spillage had accumulated along the beltline near a travelway. The note says miners walk through the area, but it does not confirm whether the belt was running, whether cleanup was underway, or whether guarding/lockout was needed.

title: loose roof bolt plate near coal travelway
hazardObservation: A roof bolt plate appeared loose near an underground coal travelway. The inspector noted rib sloughing nearby, but no fresh roof fall was observed. It is unclear whether the area was barricaded or examined by a competent person.

title: metal nonmetal underground loose ground near heading
hazardObservation: Loose material was visible on the rib near an underground heading. Employees were working in the area, but the note does not say whether scaling occurred, whether the ground was supported, or whether the area was restricted.

title: damaged trailing cable with unclear energized status
hazardObservation: A trailing cable had visible jacket damage near an underground pump. The pump area was damp. The observation does not confirm whether conductors were exposed, whether the cable was energized, or whether it had been removed from service.

title: surface highwall loose material with unclear exclusion zone
hazardObservation: Loose rock was visible on the highwall above the pit road. A haul truck route passed below the area. The note does not confirm whether the road was closed, bermed off, scaled, or examined by a competent person.

title: dump point berm appears low with truck exposure
hazardObservation: At the dump point, the berm appeared lower than the axle height of trucks using the area. The photo is angled and does not clearly show berm continuity or truck position during dumping.

title: construction trench with missing ladder access detail
hazardObservation: Workers were in a trench estimated near six feet deep. A ladder was not visible in the photo, and the note does not describe soil type, protective system, inspection, or egress distance.

title: uncapped vertical rebar near walking path
hazardObservation: Several vertical rebar ends were exposed near a construction walking path. Employees were carrying material through the area, but the note does not identify whether caps or impalement protection were available nearby.

title: crane swing radius not clearly barricaded
hazardObservation: A mobile crane was operating near a laydown area. Workers were walking near the counterweight side, and the swing radius barricade was not visible in the photo.

title: crane operating near overhead power line
hazardObservation: A crane boom was positioned near overhead utility lines. The observation does not confirm voltage, distance, de-energization, spotter use, or whether minimum approach distance was evaluated.

title: machine jam clearing with unclear energy control
hazardObservation: An employee was reaching toward a jammed packaging machine. The machine appeared stopped, but the note does not confirm whether it was locked out, blocked, or capable of unexpected movement.

title: machine guard removed but machine tagged out
hazardObservation: A machine guard was removed from a press, but the machine was tagged out for repair and no employee was working near the point of operation. The note does not confirm lock status or who controls the tag.

title: electrical panel access blocked by stored boxes
hazardObservation: Boxes were stacked in front of an electrical panel. The panel door was closed and there was no exposed wiring. The issue appears to be access and clearance, not exposed live parts.

title: forklift battery charging area with eyewash uncertainty
hazardObservation: Forklift batteries were being charged in the maintenance bay. The eyewash station was not visible and the note does not confirm ventilation, acid spill supplies, or emergency eyewash access.

title: compressed air hose damaged near workstation
hazardObservation: A compressed air hose had cracked outer covering near a workstation. It is unclear whether the hose was pressurized, leaking, restrained, or removed from service.

title: stored material secured with no employee exposure
hazardObservation: Pipe bundles were stored on a rack with chocks in place. No employees were working below or beside the rack at the time of observation. The note asks whether this is a falling-object issue.

title: closed labeled chemical containers with no leak
hazardObservation: Closed labeled chemical containers were stored in a cabinet. No leak, spill, odor, unlabeled container, or incompatible storage was observed. The inspector only noted that the cabinet looked crowded.

title: hot work permit present with controls documented
hazardObservation: Hot work was occurring in the maintenance shop. The permit was posted, nearby combustibles were cleared, an extinguisher was present, and a fire watch was assigned. The note asks whether additional evidence is needed before closing.

title: contractor shop near mine with jurisdiction uncertainty
hazardObservation: A contractor repair shop near the quarry had an unguarded bench grinder. The note does not confirm whether the shop is on mine property, whether miners use it, or whether OSHA or MSHA jurisdiction applies.

title: construction work inside operating plant with jurisdiction uncertainty
hazardObservation: A contractor crew was installing pipe supports inside an operating processing plant. Workers used a scaffold and grinders. The note does not clarify whether OSHA construction, OSHA general industry, or MSHA applies.

title: forklift traffic, spill, and electrical panel access conflict
hazardObservation: A forklift route passed through a wet spill area near an electrical panel. Pedestrians also used the aisle. The panel was closed, but boxes blocked access to it.

title: scaffold hot work with fall and fire controls unclear
hazardObservation: A welder was working from a scaffold near stored cardboard. The note does not confirm scaffold guardrails, fire watch, combustible clearance, or whether fall protection was required.

title: tank entry with hot work planned but not started
hazardObservation: A crew planned to weld inside a tank later in the shift. At the time of inspection, the manway was open and an entrant was checking tools near the opening. No atmospheric test, permit, attendant, ventilation, or hot work authorization was documented.

title: mine plant guarding issue with wet electrical area nearby
hazardObservation: A plant conveyor tail pulley had a damaged guard, and water was pooling near a motor junction box. Employees walk through the area to adjust material flow. The note does not confirm whether the junction box was energized or whether the conveyor was locked out.

title: mobile equipment repair shop with MSHA OSHA jurisdiction uncertainty
hazardObservation: A loader was parked in a maintenance shop for tire repair. The shop services mine equipment, but the note does not confirm whether the shop is on mine property, whether miners work there, whether the loader was energized, or whether the tire work involved stored-energy controls.

title: underground coal belt guard removed but area barricaded
hazardObservation: A belt drive guard was removed for scheduled maintenance, but the belt was locked out, tagged, and the entry was barricaded. No miners were working near moving parts at the time of observation. The note asks whether this should be treated as an active guarding exposure.

title: battery scoop charging with ventilation uncertainty
hazardObservation: A battery scoop was on charge in an underground shop. The area had a fan running, but the observation does not confirm hydrogen monitoring, charger condition, eyewash availability, or whether ignition sources were controlled.

title: coal mine cable hanging low near travelway
hazardObservation: A trailing cable was hanging low across the rib line near a travelway. The jacket appeared intact, but miners and mobile equipment pass through the area. The note does not confirm whether the cable was energized or protected from damage.

title: ore pass opening with unclear barricade condition
hazardObservation: An ore pass opening was observed near the drift. A chain was present but sagging, and the sign was dirty. The note does not confirm whether the opening was adequately barricaded or whether employees could access the edge.

title: refuge route sign missing with travelway open
hazardObservation: A refuge chamber route marker was missing at an intersection, but the travelway itself was open and clear. The observation does not confirm whether alternate route markers were visible or whether miners had current evacuation maps.

title: crusher dust with water spray not confirmed
hazardObservation: Dust was visible around the crusher feed during dry material processing. A water spray manifold was installed, but the note does not confirm whether sprays were operating, whether respirable dust sampling exists, or whether employees were exposed downwind.

title: conveyor emergency stop pull cord slack
hazardObservation: The emergency stop pull cord along a surface conveyor appeared slack near the tail section. The conveyor was running and a miner walks that side during cleanup. The note does not confirm whether the pull cord activates the stop switch.

title: welding cylinders in service truck with cap and securement uncertainty
hazardObservation: Oxygen and fuel gas cylinders were stored in a service truck bed near the plant. One cap was off and the cylinders were held with a loose chain. No hot work was underway.

title: construction dust cleanup with silica uncertainty
hazardObservation: Workers were dry sweeping dust after cutting concrete earlier in the day. The note does not confirm whether the material contains silica, whether wet methods or HEPA vacuum were available, or whether respirators were required.

title: ladder stored damaged but not in use
hazardObservation: A portable ladder with a bent rung was lying in the laydown yard with a damaged tag attached. No worker was using it, and it appeared staged for disposal. The note asks whether it should still be tracked.

title: telehandler load blocks forward visibility
hazardObservation: A telehandler carried a pallet of block that blocked the operator's forward view while moving through an area where laborers were staging materials. The note does not mention a spotter or traffic control.

title: excavation spoil pile near edge
hazardObservation: Spoil material was piled near the edge of an excavation. The note does not confirm excavation depth, soil type, distance from edge, or whether workers entered the trench.

title: conveyor safety mentioned only in training records
hazardObservation: The audit note says conveyor pinch point training records were incomplete. No conveyor was running, no guard was missing, and no employee exposure to moving parts was observed.

title: stored air pressure during maintenance prep
hazardObservation: Maintenance planned to replace a valve on a compressed air line. The lock was applied to the compressor disconnect, but the note does not confirm bleed-down, zero-energy verification, or whether pressure gauges were checked.

title: robot cell gate bypass rumor without confirmation
hazardObservation: An employee said the robot cell gate interlock was bypassed last week, but during inspection the gate was closed and no bypass device was visible. Production was running nearby. The note does not confirm current interlock status.

title: repetitive lifting complaint with missing weight data
hazardObservation: Employees reported shoulder strain from repeatedly lifting boxes to a shoulder-height pallet. The note does not include box weight, frequency, duration, reach distance, or injury history.

title: voluntary respirator use with unclear exposure basis
hazardObservation: Employees wore dust masks voluntarily while sweeping packaging dust. The note does not confirm contaminant type, airborne measurements, required respirator program elements, or whether the masks were required by management.

title: discarded sharp found in restroom
hazardObservation: A used needle was found in a restroom trash can. No employee injury was reported. The note does not confirm cleanup procedure, sharps container availability, PPE, or exposure control plan status.

title: powered overhead door crush point uncertainty
hazardObservation: A powered overhead door closed quickly near a pedestrian walkway. The note does not confirm photo-eye function, warning devices, manual release condition, or whether employees pass under the door during operation.

title: rail spur near mine plant with jurisdiction uncertainty
hazardObservation: A railcar was being loaded near the mine plant boundary. The note does not confirm whether the work was under mine operations, railroad control, or contractor control. Workers were near moving rail equipment.

title: haul truck crossing public road with jurisdiction uncertainty
hazardObservation: A haul truck crossed a public road between two mine areas. The note does not confirm whether traffic control was managed by the mine, DOT, or contractor personnel. Pedestrians were not observed.

title: forklift area with noise and possible carbon monoxide concern
hazardObservation: Employees complained of headaches in a busy propane forklift charging and staging area with loud backup alarms. The note does not include CO readings, ventilation status, noise dosimetry, or forklift maintenance records.

title: corrosive storage with eyewash blocked but no spill
hazardObservation: Corrosive containers were closed and labeled, but a cart partially blocked the eyewash station. No spill, leak, or splash incident was observed.

title: mine explosives magazine housekeeping with security uncertainty
hazardObservation: The explosives magazine area had weeds and debris near the perimeter fence. The magazine door appeared closed. The note does not confirm inventory, security lock condition, separation distance, or whether ignition sources were nearby.

title: photo only unclear condition requiring evidence hold
hazardObservation: The inspector uploaded a blurry photo and wrote only 'unsafe?' No task, location, equipment, employee exposure, or condition details were provided.
